questions.q: Bal Gangadhar Tilak is famous for the declaration 'Swaraj is my birthright and ___'?
questions.explanation: Tilak famously declared, 'Swaraj is my birthright and I shall have it.'

questions.q: By which popular honorific title is Bal Gangadhar Tilak best known?
questions.explanation: Tilak was honoured with the title 'Lokmanya,' meaning 'accepted by the people.'

questions.q: Which Marathi-language newspaper did Bal Gangadhar Tilak edit?
questions.explanation: Tilak edited the Marathi newspaper Kesari, along with the English paper Maratha.

questions.q: Besides Kesari, which English-language newspaper did Tilak edit?
questions.explanation: Tilak edited the English-language newspaper Maratha alongside the Marathi Kesari.

questions.q: Tilak popularized public festivals around which two figures to build national feeling?
questions.explanation: Tilak promoted public Ganesh and Shivaji festivals to foster national unity and pride.

questions.q: Tilak was a leader of which wing of the Indian National Congress?
questions.explanation: Tilak led the assertive nationalist (extremist) wing of the Congress.

questions.q: Tilak was one of the trio popularly known by which name, along with Lala Lajpat Rai and Bipin Chandra Pal?
questions.explanation: Tilak (Bal), Lala Lajpat Rai (Lal), and Bipin Chandra Pal (Pal) formed the trio Lal-Bal-Pal.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state was Bal Gangadhar Tilak born?
questions.explanation: Tilak was born in 1856 in the Ratnagiri district of present-day Maharashtra.

questions.q: Tilak strongly advocated which goal for India?
questions.explanation: Tilak made 'Swaraj,' or self-rule, the central demand of his political life.

questions.q: Tilak's festivals and newspapers were aimed primarily at building what among Indians?
questions.explanation: Tilak used festivals and the press to build national feeling and political awareness.

questions.q: Tilak was a prominent figure on the extremist side of which 1907 event that divided the Congress?
questions.explanation: Tilak led the extremists at the 1907 Surat session, where the Congress split with the moderates.

questions.q: In 1916 Tilak helped launch which organization to demand self-government for India?
questions.explanation: Tilak founded the Indian Home Rule League in 1916 to campaign for self-government.

questions.q: Tilak was tried for sedition and imprisoned for several years, serving his sentence chiefly in which prison?
questions.explanation: After his 1908 sedition trial, Tilak was imprisoned at Mandalay in Burma.

questions.q: During his imprisonment in Mandalay, Tilak wrote a major philosophical commentary on which scripture?
questions.explanation: While imprisoned, Tilak wrote the 'Gita Rahasya,' a commentary on the Bhagavad Gita.

questions.q: Tilak's lengthy commentary on the Bhagavad Gita is known by which title?
questions.explanation: Tilak's commentary is titled 'Shrimadh Bhagavad Gita Rahasya,' commonly called the Gita Rahasya.

questions.q: In which year did Bal Gangadhar Tilak die?
questions.explanation: Tilak died on 1 August 1920 in Bombay.

questions.q: Tilak was associated with founding which educational institution in Poona along with colleagues?
options: The Deccan Education Society / Fergusson College
questions.explanation: Tilak helped found the Deccan Education Society, which established Fergusson College in Poona.

questions.q: The Ganesh festival that Tilak transformed into a public celebration is observed chiefly in which region?
questions.explanation: Tilak turned the Ganesh festival into a grand public event particularly in Maharashtra and western India.

questions.q: Tilak's Shivaji festival was meant to honour the founder of which historic kingdom?
questions.explanation: The Shivaji festival commemorated Chhatrapati Shivaji, founder of the Maratha Empire.

questions.q: Within the early Congress, Tilak's assertive approach contrasted most sharply with the moderate stance of which leader?
questions.explanation: Tilak's extremism contrasted with the moderation of Gopal Krishna Gokhale, both from Maharashtra.

questions.q: Tilak's slogan 'Swaraj is my birthright' is most directly linked to his demand for what?
questions.explanation: The slogan asserted that self-rule, or Swaraj, was the natural right of Indians.

questions.q: Tilak's Home Rule League of 1916 operated alongside a parallel Home Rule League founded by which other leader?
questions.explanation: Annie Besant launched a separate Home Rule League in 1916, working in parallel with Tilak's.

questions.q: Tilak first faced a serious sedition charge in 1897 in connection with writings linked to the killing of which British official in Poona?
questions.explanation: Tilak was tried for sedition in 1897 amid the assassination of plague commissioner W. C. Rand in Poona.

questions.q: Tilak's controversial scholarly work 'The Arctic Home in the Vedas' put forward a theory about the original homeland of which people?
questions.explanation: In 'The Arctic Home in the Vedas,' Tilak argued the Vedic Aryans originated in an Arctic homeland.

questions.q: Which lawyer, later leader of the Muslim League, successfully defended Tilak in his 1916 sedition case and secured his acquittal?
questions.explanation: Muhammad Ali Jinnah defended Tilak in the 1916 sedition case, winning his acquittal on appeal before the Bombay High Court. (In the earlier 1908 trial Tilak was convicted and conducted much of his own defence.)

questions.q: Tilak's other early scholarly work, 'The Orion,' attempted to date the Vedas using evidence from what?
questions.explanation: In 'The Orion,' Tilak used astronomical references within the texts to argue for the antiquity of the Vedas.

questions.q: Tilak co-founded the New English School in Poona, which later led to the founding of which body?
questions.explanation: The New English School Tilak co-founded grew into the Deccan Education Society, which ran Fergusson College.

questions.q: Tilak's 1908 sedition conviction stemmed from articles in Kesari that the government linked to approval of violence by which Bengal revolutionaries?
options: The Muzaffarpur bombing by Khudiram Bose and Prafulla Chaki
questions.explanation: Tilak's 1908 conviction followed Kesari articles connected with the Muzaffarpur bombing carried out by Khudiram Bose and Prafulla Chaki.

questions.q: Tilak helped reunite with the moderates and shape Congress policy at which 1916 session, the year of the Lucknow Pact?
questions.explanation: At the 1916 Lucknow session the factions reunited, with Tilak playing a key role around the time of the Lucknow Pact.

questions.q: After his release from Mandalay, Tilak briefly visited Britain in 1918-1919 and there filed a libel suit against which writer?
questions.explanation: Tilak filed a libel suit in England against Valentine Chirol, who had described him in unflattering terms in his book.
